description: This figure presents the true stress versus true strain curve for Fe-Mn-Al-C austenite, illustrating its strain hardening behavior under a strain rate of 1 mm/min (as stated in the caption). The curve demonstrates a substantial capacity for work hardening, with true stress rising steeply from the origin to approximately 800 MPa at a true strain of 0.1, and continuing to increase monotonically to about 1400 MPa at a true strain near 0.37. The plot reveals an initially high hardening rate that transitions into a sustained, nearly linear hardening regime at larger strains. The accompanying text indicates that this exceptional strengthening is attributed to the austenitic structure's deformation mechanism, primarily mechanical twinning, where deformation twins act as barriers to dislocation glide and further twinning. With increasing deformation, the dislocation density increases sharply, forming localized tangles at intersections of slip systems. These microstructural evolution features underpin the alloy's high endurance under elasto-plastic cyclic loading and the invariance of absorbed energy per cycle.
